title: Event-type taxonomy + payload types + server-side session_id
description:
Create `@/lib/instrumentation/event-types.ts` and `@/lib/instrumentation/session-id.ts` (both new, greenfield). event-types.ts exports `EVENT_TYPES` as an `as const` tuple of exactly the four starter types (board_viewed, category_opened, voiceover_played, poi_clicked) mirroring PRD 1 Unit 2 AC3's DB CHECK, with `EventType` derived via `(typeof EVENT_TYPES)[number]` (not a duplicate union), a parse-guard `isEventType(x): x is EventType`, and typed payloads (`{category_id: string}` for category_opened, `{voiceover_segment: string}` for voiceover_played, none for board_viewed). A header comment documents the two-step extension boundary (migration CHECK + EVENT_TYPES bump). session-id.ts generates an opaque, non-PII, server-side session key per board-render session — not tied to IP/email/user-id, not persisted cross-session, never stored in Zustand (lives only server-side / in the events row). This is an entry unit run early in Lag 1 with no board dependency. NOTE (Moat form-decision, line 402): when this logger is actually built, decide whether `payload` carries an OPEN context envelope (`payload.context` with mode/active categories/travel_mode/time_budget/map_center/home_anchored) rather than locking event shape to a bare `{category_id}` — payload is already jsonb so keeping the shape open costs nothing, but data logged without context can never get it back.

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/13-instrumentering.md -> Unit 13.1.
acceptance_criteria: 1. EVENT_TYPES is an `as const` tuple with exactly the four starter types (board_viewed, category_opened, voiceover_played, poi_clicked), identical to PRD 1 Unit 2 AC3 CHECK set; EventType is derived via `(typeof EVENT_TYPES)[number]`, not a duplicate union. 2. A parse-guard (e.g. `isEventType(x): x is EventType`) rejects values outside the set so code never sends an event_type the DB CHECK would reject. 3. Payload types for category_opened (`{category_id: string}`) and voiceover_played (`{voiceover_segment: string}`) are typed; board_viewed has empty/no payload. 4. session-id.ts generates an opaque, non-PII value server-side (not tied to IP/email/user-id, not persisted as cross-session identifier) per §5.4; no sensitive data in Zustand — session_id lives only server-side / in the events row. 5. Header comment documents the two-step extension boundary (migration CHECK + EVENT_TYPES bump).

title: logEvent server-action (write logger)
description:
Create `@/lib/instrumentation/log-event.ts` (new, `"use server"`). Implements `logEvent(input: LogEventInput): Promise<void>` per §5.3 (eventType, optional projectId/productId/poiId/payload). Writes through `@/lib/supabase/client` `createServerClient()` (service-role) targeting the `v2` schema via `.schema('v2').from('events')` (or `db:{schema:'v2'}`) — never public.events, never `@supabase/supabase-js` directly, never client→Supabase. session_id is injected internally from Unit 1's source (caller never sets it). event_type is validated via `isEventType` before INSERT. CRITICAL: the ENTIRE body (including the `createServerClient()` lookup) is wrapped in try/catch so the fail-soft guarantee holds whether the client lookup returns null (current code) OR throws (after PRD 1 Decision 10 fail-fast fix on missing SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY). Failures are logged (not silently swallowed à la queries.ts:193) and the function returns without throwing — a logging failure must NEVER topple board render. Tier-agnostic: no reportTier parameter or branch.

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/13-instrumentering.md -> Unit 13.2.
acceptance_criteria: 1. logEvent(input: LogEventInput) (§5.3) writes through `@/lib/supabase/client` createServerClient() (service-role) targeted at the v2 schema — INSERT goes to v2.events via `.schema('v2').from('events')` (or `db:{schema:'v2'}`), never public.events (PRD 1 Unit 6 AC3); NEVER @supabase/supabase-js directly, NEVER public-client, NEVER client→Supabase. 2. session_id injected internally from Unit 1's source — caller does NOT set it. 3. Fail-soft + explicit error-handling wrapping the WHOLE body (incl. the createServerClient() lookup): try/catch so fail-soft holds both when createServerClient() returns null (current anon-fallback) AND when it throws (after PRD 1 Decision 10 / Unit 5 AC5 fail-fast fix); both outcomes (null or throw) handled — missing-service-role-key throw is logged but never topples board; a failed INSERT is caught, logged (not silently swallowed — CLAUDE.md), and the logger returns without throwing; a logging error NEVER topples board render. 4. event_type validated against isEventType (Unit 1) before INSERT. 5. No reportTier parameter or tier branch (tier-agnostic, G6). 6. `npx tsc --noEmit` 0 errors for the new files.

title: Intro-choreografi-orkestrering (welcome-beat + basic-intro + ?fly=1)
description:
Home the beat→tour mapping for the authored oval-spiral intro as pure orchestration logic on PRD 6's runIntroFlythrough primitive. Choreography logic lives in board-flythrough-orchestrator.ts (file owned/extracted by PRD 6 Unit 7; PRD 10 composes on top); intro-effect wiring touches BoardMap3D.tsx (boundary-shared per §10 Q1). Three intro owners must be AND-ed out by establishingMode (introActive = (flyMode || isWelcomeBeat || basicIntroActive) && !establishingMode) preserved VERBATIM so two animators never fight the camera pose. Three branches: basic-tier (no VO) runIntroFlythrough w/ buildBasicIntroPath landing on rest-range + dispatch END_INTRO; product-welcome (with VO) settle=WELCOME_INTRO_SETTLE_MS, fly-duration scaled to audio length, calm-sweep override, isPaused freeze; capture (?fly=1) keeps full sweep + default settle. Set window.__placyIntroFly per phase for capture sync; consume getBoardIntro(projectSlug) for per-project approach direction.

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/10-kamera-flythrough.md -> Unit 10.1.
acceptance_criteria: 1. introActive = (flyMode || isWelcomeBeat || basicIntroActive) && !establishingMode (BoardMap3D.tsx:216-217) preserved VERBATIM — two animators never fight the pose. 2. Basic-tier (no VO): basicIntroActive && !isWelcomeBeat && !flyMode → runIntroFlythrough with buildBasicIntroPath(orbitRange) (:577-590); lands on rest-range; onPhase('done') → dispatch END_INTRO; staticOnly: reducedMotion. 3. Product-welcome (with VO): settle = WELCOME_INTRO_SETTLE_MS (:597-599); fly-duration scaled Math.max(MIN_INTRO_FLY_MS, audioDurationMs - settleMs) (:600-603); calm-sweep override (WELCOME_CALM_SWEEP_DEG, ovalEccentricity: 0, :608-617); isPaused: () => audioPausedRef.current freezes without restart (:628). 4. Capture (?fly=1): keeps full sweep + default settle (:596/:607); staticOnly applies ONLY to product-beat not capture (:626-627). 5. center=target invariant preserved (introPoseAt, PRD 6) — breaking it turns oval-spiral into plain orbit. 6. window.__placyIntroFly set per phase (:583/630) — capture-sync contract (Unit 7) preserved. 7. getBoardIntro(projectSlug) (:559-562) consumed for per-project approach direction; unknown slug → {} → default intro. 8. Choreography logic is pure/hook (not JSX-bound); npx tsc --noEmit 0 errors.

title: Anchor grounding-lagringstyper (diskriminert union v1|2)
description:

ReportThemeGrounding interface with groundingVersion: 1 | 2 and v2-only optional fields (curatedNarrative/curatedAt/poiLinksUsed). Fix the stale interface docstring '(min 200 tegn)' — Zod min(1) is authoritative. Describes v2 REALITY, not v1-only.

V1Schema z.literal(1) + .passthrough() (manifest 459); V2Schema z.literal(2) with curatedNarrative min(100). 3. poiLinksUsed is z.array(z.string().min(1)) — NOT strict UUID (security via whitelist lookup, not ID form). 4. ReportThemeGroundingViewSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('groundingVersion',[V1,V2]); render-parse silent skip + server-log on mismatch. meta-asymmetry documented: V2Schema does not declare meta and is not .passthrough() so render-parse strips meta off v2 — debug-only, deliberately outside v2 render-view (PRD 5/9 must not read meta post-parse on v2). 5. title: Port adaptBoardData transform + own canonical board-types home
description:
Port adaptBoardData (ReportData → BoardData) as a pure, deterministic transform in components/variants/report/board/board-data.ts with unchanged semantics, and create the NEW canonical type-home lib/board/board-types.ts that DEFINES the three shared types BoardCategoryId, BoardPOIId, BoardAudioTrack (today inline at board-data.ts:23/31-32), re-exported from board-data.ts for a backward-compatible import surface. Transform must: filter out themes with allPOIs.length===0 (order preserved), do lead/body-concat dedup (order upperNarrative→intro→bridgeText), resolve editorial highlight-POI-IDs against the category's POIs and omit editorial when neither body nor resolved highlights exist, build poisById with LOWERCASE keys from UNFILTERED report.themes (not the empty-filtered categories set), and keep topRankedPois (score-ranked) separate from pois (distance-sorted). BoardAudioTimings is a re-export of ReportThemeAudioTimings from @/lib/types; audioTourEnabled is set but never used as gating (dead flag kept as field). Build against the project-brand TYPE-CONTRACT (getProjectBrokers, I/O-free in-memory; hoistable to shared Lag-2 like the beat-signal node) — a per-unit consume-edge, not a hard board-blocker. Transform is foto-agnostic: image/heroImage and POI photo fields pass through nullable/optional, no code path requires a photo.

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/05-board-data-state.md -> Unit 05.1.
acceptance_criteria: adaptBoardData(report) filters out themes with allPOIs.length===0 (board-data.ts:172-173), order preserved. lead/body-concat deduped against lead, order upperNarrative→intro→bridgeText (:233-250); editorial resolves highlight-POI-IDs against category POIs and is omitted when neither body nor resolved highlights exist (:262-281). poisById built with LOWERCASE keys across ALL (unfiltered) report.themes (:178-183), deliberately NOT the empty-filtered categories set, so grounding-links can resolve POIs in filtered-out themes; invariant: empty-category filter applies to navigation categories, poisById covers all POIs. topRankedPois (score-ranked, theme.topRanked) kept SEPARATE from pois (distance-sorted) (:294/:297). BoardAudioTimings re-exported from @/lib/types (:17); components import board-types from board-data, not @/lib/types. audioTourEnabled set (:209) but never used as gating (dead flag kept as field). Branded IDs (BoardCategoryId/BoardPOIId) preserved as architecture invariant. Type-home: BoardCategoryId/BoardPOIId/BoardAudioTrack DEFINED in lib/board/board-types.ts (canonical home owned here) and RE-EXPORTED from board-data.ts (backward-compatible import surface); PRD 14 imports from the type-home/re-export, this PRD does not move them to PRD 14. Foto-agnostic (note #9): transform passes image/heroImage (and POI photo fields) through nullable/optional, never assumes photo presence, no crash on missing photo; no-photo-fallback RENDERING owned by PRD 9. v2-banking: transform reads v2-targeted sources via PRD 1/3; prod-schema-snapshot.txt is column reference only. @/-prefix for @/lib/types, @/lib/board/board-types, @/lib/themes/project-brand; intra-feature ../report-data preserved.

title: Port trust-heuristikk-kjerne (Layer 1+2, ren + SSRF)
description:
Port @/lib/utils/poi-trust.ts verbatim: the pure, I/O-free calculateHeuristicTrust (BASE_SCORE 0.6, all nine scoring rules per §5.3, clamp [0,1] 2 desimaler, needsClaudeReview window 0.3-0.7), ALL_TRUST_FLAGS as single source from which TrustFlag type and VALID_TRUST_FLAGS set derive, MIN_TRUST_SCORE = 0.5 export, and the SSRF-hardened website checks. validateExternalUrl must block private-IP, bare-IP, IPv6, localhost, cloud-metadata endpoints, non-http protocol and require FQDN; checkWebsite uses redirect:'manual' with redirect-target validation (TOCTOU guard). batchValidateTrust dedups website checks per domain and runs a concurrency pool that always decrements (incl. on synchronous throw/catch) to avoid deadlock. This is the offline pure core (Fase 1) with no DB/network dependency.

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/04-trust-google-places.md -> Unit 04.1.
acceptance_criteria: 1. calculateHeuristicTrust produces identical score/flags as §5.3 table for all nine rules + clamp + needsClaudeReview window (0.3-0.7). 2. ALL_TRUST_FLAGS is sole source; TrustFlag and VALID_TRUST_FLAGS derive from it (no duplicate list). 3. validateExternalUrl blocks all §5.4 categories (private-IP, bare-IP, IPv6, localhost, metadata, non-http, non-FQDN); checkWebsite uses redirect:'manual' + validates redirect-target. 4. batchValidateTrust dedups website check per domain and runs concurrency pool without deadlock (pool always counts down, also on synchronous throw/catch). 5. Existing poi-trust tests pass unchanged (they are the acceptance source); MIN_TRUST_SCORE = 0.5 exported.

title: Port Places-enrichment-fase + lukk API-nøkkel-kontrakt
description:
Port @/lib/google-places/fetch-place-details.ts and @/lib/google-places/trust-enrichment.ts. enrichTrustSignals selects candidates where google_place_id ∧ !google_website, runs a fail-soft per-POI concurrency pool (errors collected, never aborts; pool decrements on synchronous throw). fetchPlaceDetails uses TRUST_ENRICHMENT_FIELDS (5 fields), 10s AbortController timeout, returns null when status !== 'OK'. MIGRATE fetchPlaceDetails from legacy Places key=querystring auth to Places API (New) header-auth by mirroring photo-api.ts:48-52 (X-Goog-Api-Key + X-Goog-FieldMask); this closes the CLAUDE.md rule for both call-sites (trust-enrichment.ts:91 and app/api/places/[placeId]/route.ts:74) and requires updating field mappings (TRUST_ENRICHMENT_FIELDS/DEFAULT_FIELDS) to Places-New camelCase + FieldMask. Document in file-header that facebook_url is a manual/seed field — enrichment must NOT populate it. Fix the unconditional null-coalesce of google_rating/google_review_count (trust-enrichment.ts:109-110) so refresh only overwrites when Place Details returns a value (or explicitly document refresh-to-null as intended).

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/04-trust-google-places.md -> Unit 04.2.
acceptance_criteria: 1. enrichTrustSignals selects only candidates with google_place_id ∧ !google_website; empty candidate list → no-op returning {enriched:0, failedPoiIds:[], errors:[]}. 2. Per-POI fail-soft: fetch/DB errors and 'place not found' go to errors + failedPoiIds, never stop the pool; pool decrements also on synchronous throw (deadlock guard). 3. fetchPlaceDetails uses TRUST_ENRICHMENT_FIELDS (5 fields) for enrichment, 10s timeout (AbortController), returns null when status !== 'OK'. 4. fetchPlaceDetails migrated to Places API (New) header-auth mirroring photo-api.ts:48-52 (X-Goog-Api-Key + X-Goog-FieldMask); key removed from key= querystring; closes rule for both call-sites; legacy→Places-New camelCase + FieldMask field-name shift applied. 5. facebook_url write-source clarified: no enrichment phase populates it (manual/seed migration 033 + passthrough mappings); documented in file-header that enrichment must not fill it. 6. Rating/review_count refresh overwrites only when Place Details returns a value; on undefined/missing field existing DB value preserved (no silent null-overwrite of preserved import data) — or explicitly documented in file-header that refresh-to-null is intended.

title: Port to-fase trust-pipeline-steg (enrich → re-les → score → persister)
description:
Port @/lib/pipeline/validate-report-trust.ts preserving the load-bearing ordering. Public source-POIs (no google_place_id) are deliberately skipped → skippedPublic, keep trust_score = null (= vis); manual_override-flagged and already-scored → skipped. Then: enrich (Unit 2) → re-read rows (select * in scoreIds) → score (batchValidateTrust) → persist (updatePOITrustScore). Scoring must never see the nulled import signals (google_website/business_status/price_level). Missing GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY → POIs needing enrichment are not scored, go into stillNull with a warning (no degraded score). Whole step is fail-soft per POI with no unlinking from project_pois/product_pois (filter, not delete). Return contract {scored, skipped, skippedPublic, stillNull, warnings} must match the ValidateReportTrustResult signature at validate-report-trust.ts:28-38 — this is the PRD3 consumption contract (NOT the admin-route stats form).

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/04-trust-google-places.md -> Unit 04.3.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Public source-POIs (no google_place_id) deliberately skipped → skippedPublic, keep trust_score = null (= vis); manual_override and already-scored → skipped. 2. Ordering is enrich (Unit 2) → re-read rows (select * in scoreIds) → score (batchValidateTrust) → persist (updatePOITrustScore); scoring never sees nulled import signals; ordering load-bearing especially for low/medium-reviewed legit POIs (<50 reviews) which would otherwise fall to no_website score 0.45 and be filtered out (≥200 unaffected, score 0.65). 3. Missing GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY → POIs needing enrichment not scored, placed in stillNull with warning; no degraded score. 4. title: Trust-step orchestration (two-phase) + hydration (featured + categories)
description:
Port @/lib/pipeline/validate-report-trust.ts and @/lib/pipeline/hydrate-report.ts. Place the trust step as TWO-PHASE re-enrichment BEFORE scoring — the enrichment phase (enrichTrustSignals, fetching google_website/business_status via Place Details) runs first, then scoring re-reads the rows; the two passes must NEVER be merged (Nearby Search vs Place Details is a cost/endpoint boundary). Only POIs with google_place_id are scored; public POIs keep trust_score=null; failed-enrichment POIs go to stillNull (= show, QA flag), never scored with degraded signals. Hydration: product_pois re-link (delete+re-insert), featured SET LAST (top FEATURED_TOP_N=3/category within FEATURED_MAX_DISTANCE_M=1500); replace the per-POI featured update loop with ONE batch call (.update({featured:true}).in("poi_id", ids).eq("product_id", productId)). product_categories populated with display_order from REPORT_THEME_DEFAULTS order; empty → warning. Trust heuristic imported from PRD 4. .schema('v2').

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/03-provisjon-pipeline.md -> Unit 03.6.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Order load-bearing — two passes NEVER merged: enrichment (enrichTrustSignals via Place Details) runs BEFORE scoring; scoring re-reads rows; collapsing to one pass (Place Details in Step 4) is FORBIDDEN; failed-enrichment POIs → stillNull (show, QA flag), not scored with degraded signals. 2. Only POIs with google_place_id scored; public POIs skipped, keep trust_score=null; manual_override/already-scored skipped. 3. Hydration: product_pois re-link (delete+re-insert); featured SET LAST (top FEATURED_TOP_N=3/cat within FEATURED_MAX_DISTANCE_M=1500; institutional get r=4.0/rc=10). 4. Featured marking batched: per-POI update loop replaced with one batch call (.in poi_id / .eq product_id, or upsert). 5. product_categories populated with display_order from REPORT_THEME_DEFAULTS order; empty = warning '0 av 0 steder'. 6. Trust heuristic (batchValidateTrust/enrichTrustSignals/updatePOITrustScore) imported from PRD 4, not defined here. 7. Fail-soft per POI; explicit error handling. 8. v2-targeting: trust re-read + scoring-write + product_pois/product_categories hydration use .schema('v2').

title: Generate v2 DB types + new v2 wrappers/@/lib/types
description:
Achieve TypeScript parity between the `v2` schema and app types. Generate `@/lib/supabase/database.types` from `v2` via introspection (`--schema v2`) — must include events + the 13 keeper tables, none of the 11 legacy tables. Re-derive `@/lib/types` against v2, trimming all dead Trip/Guide/scroll symbols (StorySection L110, ThemeStory L124, ThemeStorySection L133, Trip/TripStop/ProjectTrip L801/790/846, TripConfig/TripStopConfig, TripCategory/TripDifficulty/TripSeason L739/752/754, TripStopId L701, createTripStopId L711, fields activeThemeStory/themeStories/trails; verify whether TrailCollection L167 trims with the Trip-stack). Write new `v2`-targeting wrappers in `@/lib/supabase/queries`/`mutations` (via `.schema('v2')` / `db: { schema: 'v2' }`) for the board-MVP path, with explicit error-handling (no silent `return []` swallow — at least log errors). Trimming the old public-rooted code (54 legacy refs) is cutover, NOT a baseline requirement; old and new wrappers coexist until Unit 3.

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/01-datamodell-supabase.md -> Unit 01.6.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `@/lib/supabase/database.types` generated from v2 schema (`--schema v2`), includes events + 13 keeper tables, none of the 11 legacy tables. 2. `@/lib/types` has no references to dead symbols (StorySection, ThemeStory, ThemeStorySection, Trip/TripStop/ProjectTrip, TripConfig/TripStopConfig, TripCategory/TripDifficulty/TripSeason, TripStopId, createTripStopId, activeThemeStory/themeStories/trails); verify whether TrailCollection trims with Trip-stack. 3. v2-wrappers target v2 via `.schema('v2')`/`db: { schema: 'v2' }` for the board-MVP path; trimming old public legacy-refs is cutover not baseline. 4. New v2-wrappers preserve/improve explicit error-handling on each Supabase call — no inherited silent swallow without logging. 5. New v2-wrappers compile against v2 types; board-MVP path reads/writes against v2. 6. `npx tsc --noEmit` 0 errors; `npm run lint` 0 errors; `npm test` passes.

title: Run v2 baseline via psql + verification
description:
Run the v2 baseline against the database via psql (NOT `supabase db push` — NNN numbering incompatible), expose `v2` in the Supabase API, and verify column existence + RLS against `v2`. Write `@/docs/rebuild/baseline-migration-runbook.md` as the run-and-verify runbook. `public` is untouched so no backup/data-protection. Add `v2` to Settings → API → Exposed schemas and verify GRANT USAGE/table grants to anon/authenticated/service_role. Post-flight REST column-existence checks against v2 (using `Accept-Profile: v2` header), test-INSERT into `v2.events` via service-role REST, and a two-direction RLS smoke test (anon cannot write any v2 table nor read non-display-ready place_knowledge; service-role via createServerClient with real SERVICE_ROLE_KEY CAN read non-display-ready place_knowledge — confirms the Unit 5 key-fix). Demo-parity validation (bonus E2E test that also gates Unit 3): the three reference boards (Wesseløkka/Stasjonskvartalet/Ranheim) are RE-PROVISIONED into v2 via PRD 3 (not migrated from public).

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/01-datamodell-supabase.md -> Unit 01.7.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Migration run with psql against pooler-URL; `supabase db push` NOT used; additive (`CREATE SCHEMA v2` + `CREATE TABLE v2.*`), public untouched. 2. v2 added to Settings → API → Exposed schemas, GRANT USAGE/table grants to anon/authenticated/service_role verified; no pg_dump backup or pre/post row-count needed. 3. Post-flight REST column-existence check against v2 for each critical field returns 200 with the fields (`Accept-Profile: v2`). 4. Demo-parity validation: the three reference boards (Wesseløkka/Stasjonskvartalet/Ranheim) re-provisioned into v2 via PRD 3 (not migrated from public) and render with demo-parity — E2E test of schema+RLS+wrappers, and the gate for the later public-drop (Unit 3). 5. `v2.events` accepts a test-INSERT via service-role REST. 6. RLS smoke test both directions against v2: (a) anon CANNOT write any v2 table and CANNOT read non-display-ready `v2.place_knowledge`; (b) service-role (createServerClient with real SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) CAN read non-display-ready `v2.place_knowledge`.

title: Skriv to-nivå-modell + kondisjonell-render-kontrakt-dokument
description:
Lag nytt dokument @/docs/rebuild/tier-kjerne-vs-overflate.md som normativt definerer de to leveransenivåene (nivå 1 = autonomt generert default; nivå 2 = eksplisitt kuratert editorial, admin-only i starten), det delte kjerne-vs-overflate-skillet, og kondisjonell-render-kontrakt-tabellen fra §5.2 (nivå 2 + de fem ortogonale flaggene 3D/VO/camera/brokers/brand med driver + hva som vises). Dokumentet er kontrakten som provisjon (PRD 3) og render (PRD 9) er enige om — selve renderingen bygges ikke her. Slår fast at aksene er ortogonale (ingen akse gater en annen), at det IKKE finnes render-gating på reportTier som skjuler features (patch #2), at nivå-modellen er segment-agnostisk (ingen segment-felt), og at profil (bolig/naering) er en akse ortogonal til nivå. Inkluder lazy-load-noten for de tre verifiserte tunge ortogonale modulene (reels/splash-video, voiceover-orchestration, kuratert hero-asset).

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/02-tier-capability-manifest.md -> Unit 02.1.
acceptance_criteria: Dokumentet definerer de to nivåene: nivå 1 = autonomt generert default (alle board med mindre annet bestilles); nivå 2 = eksplisitt kuratert editorial (admin-only i starten). Dokumentet lister DELT kjerne (3D-motor, RSC-render, taksonomi fra report-defaults, kamera-rammeverk, board-skall, Auto/Fri/Kart/3D-kontroller) eksplisitt som «alltid lastet, alltid identisk». Dokumentet inneholder kondisjonell-render-kontrakt-tabellen fra §5.2 (nivå 2 + de fem ortogonale flaggene: 3D/VO/camera/brokers/brand) med driver + hva som vises — og slår fast at aksene er ortogonale (ingen akse gater en annen). Dokumentet inneholder lazy-load-noten: de tre tunge ortogonale modulene (reels/splash-<video>, voiceover-orchestration, kuratert hero-asset) dynamic()-importeres så nivå-1/uten-flagg ikke betaler bundle-kost; faktisk wrapping + bundle-bevis eies av PRD 9; kun de tre verifiserte modulene. Dokumentet slår fast «ingen render-gating på reportTier som skjuler features» (patch #2), at nivå-modellen IKKE har segment-felt (segment-agnostisk), og at profil (bolig/naering) er en akse ORTOGONAL til nivå.

description:

acceptance_criteria: ReportTierSchema = z.union([z.literal(1), z.literal(2)]); OptionalReportTierSchema.safeParse(3), .safeParse("2"), .safeParse(0) feiler, .safeParse(2) og .safeParse(1) lykkes; undefined = nivå 1. validateReportTier produserer KUN invalid-tier (error, tidlig-retur), highlight-poi (warning, hoppes hvis poiIds utelatt) og editorial (error ved nivå 2 — tom themes eller tema uten editorial). Funn er data, ingen throws. De fem fjernede sjekkene (vo/camera-tours/has3d-addon/brokers/brand-assets) finnes IKKE lenger i readiness-laget; report-tier.ts importerer ikke lenger getProjectBrokers. title: RLS policies on v2.* + client key-contract fix
description:
Add the RLS section to `070_baseline.sql` targeting `v2.*` tables, plus fix the client key fallback in `@/lib/supabase/client`. Enable RLS on all 14 v2 tables (13 keeper + events). Read policy: publish-ready board data (pois, products, projects, areas, categories, category_slugs, translations, product_pois, product_categories, project_pois) readable by anon; `v2.place_knowledge` anon-read gated on `display_ready = true`; `generation_requests` (PII/consent) service-role-only. Write policy: INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on all v2 tables service-role only; `v2.events` INSERT-only for service-role. Fix the verified bug at `client.ts:30` where `createServerClient()` falls back to the anon key when `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` is missing — preferred fix (a): remove the anon fallback so it fail-fast throws in server runtime (default). Requires v2 exposed in Supabase API + GRANT USAGE/table grants (handled in Unit 7).

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/01-datamodell-supabase.md -> Unit 01.5.
acceptance_criteria: 1. RLS enabled (`ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`) on all 14 v2 tables (13 keeper + events); all policy targets are `v2.<navn>`. 2. Read policy: publish-ready board data readable for anon (requires v2 exposed + GRANT USAGE/table grants). 3. Write policy: INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on all v2 tables service-role only; `v2.events` INSERT-only for service-role, no anon write. 4. `v2.place_knowledge` read policy gates anon on `display_ready = true`. 5. Client-key-contract: fix `createServerClient()` anon-fallback at `client.ts:30` that silently breaks service-role write policy, events INSERT-only, and admin read of non-display-ready place_knowledge (`getAllKnowledgeAdmin`, `queries.ts:1606`) — choose (a) fail-fast throw when SERVICE_ROLE_KEY missing in server runtime (default), or (b) keep fallback + runtime-assert + documented env-key-contract.

title: Kjør seed mot produksjon + manuell verifikasjon + screenshots
description: Siste steg: faktisk kjøre seed-scriptet mot produksjons-Supabase og verifisere at Wesselsløkka-rapporten viser ny oppsummeringsseksjon korrekt. Steg: (1) Hent megler-data manuelt fra wesselslokka.no/kontakt — navn, tittel, telefon, e-post, foto-URL. Hvis ikke tilgjengelig, bruk placeholder med DEMO-merking. (2) Oppdater data/wesselslokka-summary.ts med reelt innhold. (3) Kjør seed-scriptet i --dry-run mode, inspiser diff manuelt. (4) Hvis diff ser riktig ut, kjør --apply. (5) Verifiser auto-check fra scriptet går igjennom. (6) Åpne http://localhost:3000/eiendom/broset-utvikling-as/wesselslokka/rapport i browser, ta screenshot av ny oppsummeringsseksjon. (7) Klikk 'Meld interesse' og verifiser at wesselslokka.no/kontakt åpnes i ny fane. (8) Klikk tel:-link og verifiser telefon-dialog. (9) Klikk share-ikonet og verifiser URL-kopi + ikon-swap. (10) Verifiser Plausible events i dashboard hvis tilgjengelig. (11) Ta før/etter-screenshots av hele rapporten. Se Kort 6 akseptansekriterier i plan.
acceptance_criteria: Megler-data hentet og lagt inn (eller DEMO-merket placeholder); dry-run diff verifisert; apply kjørt uten feil; auto-verifikasjon passerte; rapporten viser alle fem blokk-elementer (headline, insights, broker-invite, broker-card, primary-CTA); 'Meld interesse' åpner wesselslokka.no i ny fane; tel:-link fungerer; share-ikon kopierer URL; Plausible-events registreres; screenshots tatt av ny tilstand
close_reason: Seed applied successfully mot prod-Supabase. Post-write verifikasjoner passerte (data + HTTP). Visuell verifikasjon via browser: alle 5 blokk-elementer rendrer (headline, 5 insights med Sparkles, varm broker-invite, broker-kort med fallback User-ikon + tel/mail, Meld interesse primær-CTA, tel:-link under, share-ikon øverst). CTA-lenker verifisert: Meld interesse -> wesselslokka.no/kontakt target=_blank, tel: normalisert til +4773871500, mailto: med encoded subject. Footer intakt. Commit laget med alle endringer. Backup tilgjengelig for rollback. Merknad: megler er placeholder (DEMO-merket) — må erstattes med reell Heimdal-megler før salgsmøte.

title: Seed-script framework: backup + dryrun + whitelist + concurrency
description: scripts/seed-wesselslokka-summary.ts — PATCH products.config JSONB med summary/brokers/cta. Må ha: (1) --dry-run som default, --apply for faktisk write, (2) pre-write backup til backups/products-{id}-{ts}.json, (3) whitelist-sjekk av eksisterende reportConfig-nøkler (avbryt hvis ukjente), (4) concurrency guard via updated_at=eq.{value} i PATCH-URL, (5) shallow merge (spread) så themes/trails/heroIntro bevares, (6) post-write data-verifikasjon: deep-equal mot backup for bevarte felter + eksistens av nye felter, (7) post-write HTTP-verifikasjon: GET rapport-URL, assert 200 og headline i HTML. Legg backups/ i .gitignore. Wesselsløkka-innhold hentes fra ny fil data/wesselslokka-summary.ts som TypeScript-konstant. Se Kort 6 i plan for full kode.
acceptance_criteria: Scriptet default er --dry-run; pre-write backup skrives til backups/; whitelist avbryter ved ukjente nøkler; concurrency guard bruker updated_at i PATCH-URL; shallow merge bevarer themes/trails/heroIntro; post-write data-assert: nye felter til stede + bevarte felter deep-equal; post-write HTTP-assert: 200 + headline i HTML; .gitignore oppdatert; data/wesselslokka-summary.ts opprettet med type-safe content
close_reason: scripts/seed-wesselslokka-summary.ts + data/wesselslokka-summary.ts opprettet. Dry-run testet mot prod-Supabase: fant riktig product (c87b51f6-9cf7-4738-b452-cbea0bb62c65), whitelist OK (eksisterende keys: themes/trails/heroIntro), diff viser at brokers/cta/summary legges til mens eksisterende bevares. Backup skrevet til backups/. Tabellnavn rettet til 'projects' (ikke project_containers). Default --dry-run, --apply for å skrive. Concurrency guard via updated_at i PATCH URL. Post-write data-verify + HTTP-verify.

title: Emit-site spec (where the four calls go)
description:
Create `@/docs/rebuild/instrumentering-emit-sites.md` (new). DECLARE the four emit-sites as a spec, separated from implementation (Unit 5 / PRD 9), so PRD 9 and PRD 13 don't collide on board-component files (Agent-Teams rule). Lists each emit-site with its verified anchor: poi_clicked → handlePOIClick (BoardMap3D.tsx:466, dispatch :471), category_opened → SELECT_CATEGORY dispatch from a UI handler (action-type board-state.tsx:43; board-state.tsx owns the dispatch boundary :124/:152 but not the call-site — canonical emit point is one concrete handler, resolve with PRD 5/9 so two sites don't double-count), voiceover_played → VO-play-trigger (PRD 9, unverified in rebuild-skall — mark 'resolve with PRD 9'; explicitly NOT hasVoiceOver:261 which is only a content-exists boolean), board_viewed → board-mount (PRD 9). Spec mandates fire-and-forget (not await-blocking), tier-agnostic, and the explicit call-pattern `void logEvent(...).catch(() => {})` to prevent unhandled floating-promise rejection (a known Next.js pitfall with un-awaited 'use server' calls). Maps each emit-site to which events fields/payload it fills, and points to PRD 9 as owner of the components where calls are placed (Unit 5).

Full spec: docs/rebuild/prd/13-instrumentering.md -> Unit 13.4.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Spec lists the four emit-sites with verified anchor (per §4 table): poi_clicked → handlePOIClick (BoardMap3D.tsx:466, dispatch :471), category_opened → SELECT_CATEGORY-dispatch from UI handler (action-type board-state.tsx:43; board-state.tsx owns dispatch boundary :124/:152 but not the call-site), voiceover_played → VO-play-trigger (PRD 9, unverified-in-rebuild-skall — marked 'resolve with PRD 9'; NOT hasVoiceOver:261 which is a content-exists boolean), board_viewed → board-mount (PRD 9). 2. Spec states each call is fire-and-forget (not await-blocking on render path) and tier-agnostic (same on all tiers, no reportTier branch); spec specifies the call pattern explicitly: an un-awaited 'use server' call from a client handler is a known Next.js pitfall (floating promise → possible unhandled rejection, React doesn't guarantee completion on fast unmount/nav), so the pattern is `void logEvent(...).catch(() => {})` (or equivalent .catch-bound) so a rejection is NEVER unhandled; logEvent must also be robust to being called-and-abandoned (no resource needing cleanup). 3. Spec maps each emit-site to which events field/payload it fills (poi_clicked → poi_id + payload.category_id; category_opened → payload.category_id; voiceover_played → payload.voiceover_segment; board_viewed → board context only). 4. Spec explicitly points to PRD 9 as owner of the components where logEvent calls are actually placed (Unit 5).

title: Aggregation / read layer (engagement statistics)
description:

Reads from v2.events via `.schema('v2').from('events')` (never public.events). Explicit error-handling through createServerClient() — a failed fetch gives a clear error + logging, NOT silent empty stats (no `return []` swallowing à la queries.ts:193). Server-side only (called from server component/action, never useEffect-fetch). Can be built in parallel with Unit 4/5 (reads the table, not the emit-sites). COORDINATION: PRD 1 Unit 6 (v2-wrappers; legacy public-rooted queries.ts trim is cutover not baseline) should be landed/coordinated before adding getEngagementStats so the two don't collide on @/lib/supabase/queries.ts.

scripts/gemini-grounding.ts (tsx-shebang, --apply/--dry-run/--force)

Script-flow: backup → parallell Gemini (Promise.allSettled) → parallel URL-resolve (p-limit=5) → totalfeil-abort (>=5/7) → deep-merge PATCH med whitelist + optimistic lock (updated_at) → post-write deep-equal → revalidateTag.

Type: ReportThemeGrounding med searchEntryPointHtml (Google ToS-påkrevd, DOMPurify-sanert), groundingVersion: z.literal(1), sources[], meta (debug).

Golden pattern: scripts/seed-wesselslokka-summary.ts (backup, whitelist, concurrency lock, post-write check).

Nye deps: react-markdown rehype-sanitize isomorphic-dompurify p-limit ipaddr.js

Ikke-scope: UI (#3), skill-endringer (#2).

- SSRF-tester: avviser 127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, 192.168.1.1, ::1
- Totalfeil: 5/7 fail → exit 2, ingen PATCH
